description: Analyze the conformational difference between active and inactive kinase states using PyMOL. Fetch the structures of BRAF bound to Vemurafenib (PDB: 3OG7) and Sorafenib (PDB: 1UWH). Align the two structures to superimpose the kinase domains. Highlight the DFG motif (residues 594-596) to visualize the 'DFG-in' vs 'DFG-out' flip. Measure the distance between the C-alpha (CA) atoms of Phe595 in the aligned 3OG7 and 1UWH structures. Render a high-resolution PNG figure showing both structures with the DFG motif highlighted, and save it to ~/PyMOL_Data/images/braf_dfg_flip.png. Write a structural analysis report to ~/PyMOL_Data/braf_inhibition_report.txt containing: 1) The explicitly measured distance between the two Phe595 C-alpha atoms in Angstroms, 2) Identification of which PDB ID represents the active 'DFG-in' state and which represents the inactive 'DFG-out' state, and 3) A brief explanation of how Sorafenib exploits the DFG-out conformation (mentioning the allosteric or hydrophobic pocket).
